rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK SPENCER III VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Re-Open Plaintiffâ¿¿S Deposition For The Limited Purpose Of Deposing Plaintiff By Videotape Regarding The Declaration Submitted In Opposition To Rockwell Automation, Inc.Â¿¿S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, March 6, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.'s motion to re-open plaintiff's deposition for the limited purpose of deposing plaintiff regarding the declaration submitted in opposition to its motion for summary judgment is denied. Although the relevant law was not cited or discussed by defendant, plaintiff's deposition may only be re-opened for good cause shown. (C.C.P. 2025.610.) Defendant failed to demonstrate such good cause as it failed to properly question plaintiff during his deposition. Further, even if defendant had demonstrated good cause, the motion would still be denied because C.C.P. 2024.050, the statute upon which defendant moves and which is necessary here because discovery is closed, states that a motion brought under this section "shall be accompanied by" a meet and confer declaration. Defendant did not submit such declaration nor did it show that it attempted to resolve this issue informally with plaintiff's counsel prior to filing this motion. Defendant violated C.C.P. 2024.050. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requiring John Cahill, Jr., And Archer Norris Aplc To Pay The Costs And Fees For The Failure To Appear At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, March 6, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs' motion for an order requiring John Cahill, Jr. and Archer Norris to pay the costs and fees for the failure to appear at deposition is granted. There is no substantial justification why Mr. Cahill failed to appear at deposition pursuant to a subpoena. Sanctions of $500 are awarded against Mr. Cahill. Sanctions of $3,203 are awarded against Archer Norris. The initial opposition of Cahill Construction Company, Inc. was timely. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
